title: penstemon guadalupensis
intro: Penstemon guadalupensis, also known as the Guadalupe beardtongue, is a perennial plant species native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, with medicinal properties including the ability to reduce fever, soothe wounds, and alleviate respiratory issues. The health benefits of Penstemon guadalupensis include its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce inflammation, and act as an expectorant to relieve congestion. The herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by steeping the leaves and flowers in hot water or mixing with a solvent like ethanol.
